summary: Russian officials claim American humanoid robots (Phantom MK-1) have been deployed to Ukraine and are already engaging Russian forces, sparking concern among Russian military observers. On the battlefield, Ukrainian forces are employing advanced FPV drone tactics, including using log-periodic directional antennas on "Molniyas" to increase range and resist jamming, while also experimenting with pre-strike maneuvers like throwing enemy riders off horses. Notable incidents include a Russian soldier charging an FPV drone with a bayonet and losing, and another being struck by two drones simultaneously while hiding behind a tree. Additionally, Iranian-backed militias launched a fiber-optic FPV drone attack on a US base in Iraq, and the Rubikon Center reported eliminating 110 Ukrainian targets across multiple fronts on March 18, 2026.
